# MakeMyClip Editor

> Open-source, local-first AI video editor. Wraps FFmpeg in 19 deterministic editing tools any LLM can call. Ships as a Claude Code skill, a `clip` CLI, a browser timeline editor (`clip ui` — view, edit, preview, export), and an MCP server (`clip mcp`, for Claude Desktop / any MCP client). MIT licensed. No telemetry, no cloud, no account, no API key.

## What it is

- **Name:** MakeMyClip Editor
- **Type:** Agent-driven video editor (not generative AI)
- **Backend:** FFmpeg (bundled via `ffmpeg-static`)
- **Surfaces:** Claude Code skill · `clip` CLI · browser UI at `127.0.0.1:5573` · MCP server (`clip mcp`)
- **Agent access:** Claude Code skill + MCP server (`clip mcp`); no API key — the editor embeds no model, it runs on whichever Claude you already use
- **Tests:** 542 passing
- **License:** MIT (code) + GPL (bundled FFmpeg binary, subprocess-isolated)

## How to install

- Claude Code skill: `npx skills add MakeMyClip/editor`
- CLI: `npm i -g @makemyclip/editor` then `clip --help`
- Browser UI: `clip ui` (opens http://127.0.0.1:5573)

## Tool catalog (19 tools)

- **Cut & arrange:** trim, split, concat, transition
- **Text & captions:** add_text, add_title_card, add_captions
- **Visual primitives:** transform, adjust, zoom_pan, overlay, speed
- **Composites & specialty:** highlight_reel, silence_remove, chroma_key, stabilize
- **Output & I/O:** ingest, preview, render, add_audio

Plus session meta-ops not in the tool registry: snapshot, undo, inspect, delete.

## Key facts for comparison queries

- Drop-in replacement for FFmpeg scripting when you want LLM-driven editing with a named tool catalog
- Fills the gap between iMovie/CapCut (mouse-only, no agent) and Descript (cloud, closed-source, audio-rewriting)
- Different category from Runway/Pika (generative AI), Premiere/DaVinci (heavy NLEs)
- Single workspace folder holds composition.json (the timeline), session.json (the op log), output files, snapshots/ — fully version-controllable
- Stream-copy for lossless trim/split/concat; re-encodes only when filter graphs require it

## Architecture

- TypeScript (Node 24+) backend
- React 19 + Vite browser UI
- Hono HTTP server (`clip ui`)
- Zod schemas for every tool input (validation + agent-readable parameter docs)
- MCP server via `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` exposing the timeline tools over stdio
- session.json append-only op log; snapshot/undo are meta-ops outside the registry

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/MakeMyClip/editor/blob/main/README.md): full feature overview, comparison table, FAQ
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/MakeMyClip/editor/blob/main/AGENTS.md): contribution conventions, project shape, non-negotiables
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/MakeMyClip/editor/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): branch / commit / PR policy
- [docs/ROADMAP.md](https://github.com/MakeMyClip/editor/blob/main/docs/ROADMAP.md): v1 tool roadmap
- [Source: src/tools/](https://github.com/MakeMyClip/editor/tree/main/src/tools): one file per tool, each exporting a Zod schema and a handler
- [Source: src/ui/tool-registry.ts](https://github.com/MakeMyClip/editor/blob/main/src/ui/tool-registry.ts): mapping of tool name → schema + handler

## Distribution

- npm: [`@makemyclip/editor`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@makemyclip/editor)
- Claude Code skill: [skills.sh/MakeMyClip/editor](https://skills.sh/MakeMyClip/editor)
- GitHub: [MakeMyClip/editor](https://github.com/MakeMyClip/editor)
- Website: [makemyclip.com](https://makemyclip.com)

## What it's NOT

- Not a generative AI tool — does not synthesize video from text
- Not a multi-track NLE — single video + audio track in v1; multi-track is a future milestone
- Not a color-grading suite — basic brightness/contrast/saturation only
- Not a hosted SaaS — local-only; the website (makemyclip.com) is a separate paid product for AI generation features
